finding.next_action: Expand inventory via additional city municipal codes/ordinances, association directories (e.g., International Downtown Association, Association of Idaho Cities), and municipal GIS/open data portals for remaining cities (e.g., Twin Falls, Meridian status, Sandpoint current status); seek any boundary GeoJSON/shapefiles.
finding.notes: Enabling authority verified as Idaho Code Title 50 Chapter 26 (Business Improvement Districts, authorized 1980, purposes include parking, public space improvements, events, transportation, retail promotion; special assessments on businesses/business property; cities may contract for administration). Distinct from broader Local Improvement Districts (LIDs, Title 50 Chapter 17 for capital infrastructure like streets/utilities, financed by assessments/bonds). Separate limited provision at 50-1703A (Local Business Improvement Districts, 1987) for exterior building improvements to match city architectural themes (requires 100% parcel owner consent, assessed as LIDs, max 20% of market value). No statewide registry, inventory, or central GIS of BIDs located after statute, government, and open-data searches (failed search ≠ none exist). Known active BIDs per official/district sources in Boise (est. ~1987), Nampa (~1985), Caldwell, Idaho Falls (~1990s, renewal 2027), Coeur d'Alene (1990), Lewiston (2020), Pocatello (1991); Sandpoint had one historically (dissolution attempt ~2010 failed; current status unclear/possibly inactive per some planning docs). Meridian has discussed/proposed but documents note operating without one. DBA notes 'at least six' in Idaho (likely undercount). Boundaries available as maps/PDFs on several district/city pages but no statewide or easily downloadable GeoJSON/ArcGIS layers confirmed. Distinctions from urban renewal/URDs and other special districts observed. Candidate sources are research leads only.
